name: La Casita del Duende – round trip from Villanófar
name_original: La Casita del Duende – ida y vuelta desde Villanófar
region_intro: Hiking trails around Gradefes, a municipality in León, Spain, traverse a landscape shaped by the Esla River and its diverse ecosystems. The region features riparian forests, holm oak groves, and oak woodlands, offering varied natural environments. Terrain ranges from gentle river paths to more challenging routes in the mountainous area of Riaño. These natural features provide a backdrop for both outdoor activity and cultural exploration. Best hiking trails around Gradefes The most popular hiking route is Pico Los Rejos, Cecilio viewpoint and Ermita de San Guillermo de Peñacorada – circular from Cistierna, a 6.7 miles (10.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 19 minutes to complete. This moderate route includes significant elevation gain, leading to scenic viewpoints. Another top favourite among local hikers is La Casita del Duende – round trip from Villanófar, an easy 2.9 miles (4.7 km) path. This trail offers a gentle walk through the local countryside, suitable for all fitness levels. Local hikers also love the Villafañe-Lancia PRC-LE 66 Trail, a 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail leading through varied rural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 41 minutes. Hiking around Gradefes is defined by river valleys, forested areas, and rolling hills, with routes often incorporating historical sites. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more demanding ascents. The routes in Gradefes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 250 hikers have used komoot to explore Gradefes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Gradefes?
A: Gradefes offers over a dozen hiking routes, catering to various skill levels. You'll find everything from gentle riverside strolls to more demanding ascents in the surrounding hills.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Gradefes has several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047729431/la-casita-del-duende-round-trip-from-villanofar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Casita del Duende – round trip from Villanófar&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.9-mile (4.7 km) path that offers a gentle walk through the local countryside. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047165601/villafane-lancia-prc-le-66-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villafañe-Lancia PRC-LE 66 Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.8 miles (6.1 km) long and leads through varied rural landscapes.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Gradefes?
A: Hiking in Gradefes offers a diverse range of natural landscapes, primarily shaped by the Esla River. You'll traverse riparian forests, holm oak groves, and oak woodlands. The region is also part of the mountainous area of Riaño, providing scenic vistas and varied terrain, from river valleys to rolling hills.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Gradefes area?
A: Yes, several routes in Gradefes are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A notable example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050036489/pico-los-rejos-cecilio-viewpoint-and-ermita-de-san-guillermo-de-penacorada-circular-from-cistierna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Los Rejos, Cecilio viewpoint and Ermita de San Guillermo de Peñacorada – circular from Cistierna&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 6.7-mile (10.7 km) trail with significant elevation gain leading to scenic viewpoints.

Q: What historical or cultural sites can I explore while hiking?
A: Gradefes offers a unique blend of outdoor activity and cultural immersion. Routes like the 'Ruta de los Monasterios' integrate historical sites into the natural surroundings. You can visit landmarks such as the Santa María de Gradefes Monastery, the Mozarabic Church of San Miguel de Escalada, and the ruins of the San Pedro de Eslonza Monastery, often incorporated into longer walking tours.

Q: Are there trails that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. The region's position in the mountainous area of Riaño means many trails offer scenic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050036489/pico-los-rejos-cecilio-viewpoint-and-ermita-de-san-guillermo-de-penacorada-circular-from-cistierna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Los Rejos, Cecilio viewpoint and Ermita de San Guillermo de Peñacorada – circular from Cistierna&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, is known for leading to significant viewpoints.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Gradefes?
A: Gradefes is generally pleasant for hiking during spring and autumn when temperatures are mild and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can also be suitable, especially for early morning or late afternoon hikes, while winter might offer different challenges and beauty, particularly in higher elevations.

Q: What wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The diverse habitats around the Esla River, including riparian forests and oak woodlands, are home to a range of local fauna. Hikers might have opportunities for wildlife observation, potentially spotting animals such as wolves, deer, and various birds of prey.

Q: Are there any specific attractions or landmarks along the routes?
A: Yes, several interesting landmarks can be found near or along the hiking routes. These include historical bridges like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/917498" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Villarente&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1000787" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Esla River Bridge, Mansilla de las Mulas&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter unique natural features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6771999" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reliegos Meteorite Impact Site&lt;/a&gt;, or cultural markers like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6756922" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Santiago Distance Marker&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Gradefes?
A: The routes in Gradefes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the natural beauty of the Esla River ecosystem, and the blend of outdoor activity with cultural exploration.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for experienced trekkers seeking a challenge, Gradefes offers more demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31468111" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñacorada Summit loop from Cistierna&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is a difficult 10.1-mile (16.3 km) hike with significant elevation gain, providing a strenuous but rewarding experience.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay overnight in Gradefes?
A: Gradefes and its surrounding villages offer options for refreshments and accommodation. While hiking, you might come across local establishments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2926583" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bar Elvis&lt;/a&gt;, and there are various cafes, pubs, and accommodation choices in the area for those looking to extend their stay.
tips: Beautiful wooden bridge that we find on the route to La Casita del Duende. The bridge allows us to get closer to the Cuchurrumaldo spring and the orchard.
One of the main elements of the route of La Casita del Duende. A corner that the little ones will love, and the not so little ones.

At this point of the La Casita del Duende route, we find a picnic area and a hut, it is an ideal place to rest and recover energy since we only have to turn around and return by the same path to Villanófar.

waypoints.name: Shelter and Picnic Area on La Casita del Duende Route
